summary: Eugene and Ruth Lasartemay co-founded a private collection in 1946 that became the African American Museum and Library at Oakland, preserving Black history through artifacts, documents, and oral histories. Their initiative laid the foundation for a public institution dedicated to African American culture and memory in the East Bay.
keywords: African American Museum and Library at Oakland
locationLabel: 14th Street location of the African American Museum and Library at Oakland

claims.object: the initial private collection that became the African American Museum and Library at Oakland

claims.predicate: began collecting artifacts and documents in 1946
claims.object: a private collection focused on African American history
the East Bay Negro Historical Society in 1965

historicalContext: The founding of the AAMLO in 1946 emerged during the early years of the modern civil rights movement, a period when Black communities increasingly sought to preserve their own histories outside mainstream institutions. This effort reflects broader trends of grassroots archiving and cultural self-determination in response to systemic exclusion from established historical narratives.
